# OpenClaw Hands-on Training

A hands-on training covering OpenClaw fundamentals, production deployment, and everything you need to build a profitable freelance service around it.

💵 $200
⏱ 20 Hours
📅 5 Days
🎯 Guru · Freelancer · Upwork

Prefer to skip the training and have it done for you? [**Hire us for OpenClaw Support**](/linux-ai-services/openclaw-ai-services/) instead.

## What Is OpenClaw Hands-on Training?

OpenClaw Hands-on Training is School of Freelancing's 20-hour, instructor-led course on OpenClaw — an open-source autonomous AI agent platform that functions as a 24/7 personal digital assistant. Over 5 days it takes trainees from OpenClaw fundamentals through production deployment, configuration, and troubleshooting, then shows them how to structure that skill as a paid freelance service. It's built for beginners and career-changers who want to specialize in OpenClaw deployment and support.

## Key Takeaways

- **Format:** Online, instructor-led, hands-on training
- **Duration:** 20 hours over 5 days
- **Price:** $200 USD, paid in full before enrollment
- **Covers:** OpenClaw fundamentals and core architecture, production deployment, configuring and maintaining instances for client workloads, troubleshooting common deployment issues, and structuring OpenClaw work as a freelance service
- **Best for:** Beginners and career-changers who want to specialize in OpenClaw and freelance on Upwork, Freelancer.com, and Guru.com

## What You'll Learn

- OpenClaw fundamentals and core architecture
- Deploying OpenClaw into production-style environments
- Configuring and maintaining OpenClaw instances for real client workloads
- Troubleshooting common OpenClaw deployment issues
- Structuring OpenClaw deployment and support as a paid freelance service

## Who This Is For

This training suits beginners and career-changers who want to specialize in OpenClaw and can commit to 20 hours of hands-on, instructor-led training over 5 days, and who meet the
[system & participation requirements](/contact-us/) (Ubuntu Desktop 24.04/26.04 LTS PC, reliable fiber-optic internet, and a valid government-issued ID).
